title: Olytic Session Summary
description: Canonical schema for episodic session memory content. This is the CONTENT payload — it is wrapped in vault-entry-schema.json before being written to the vault. Skills read this file at runtime at SessionClose — do not bake field definitions into plugin source.
read_timing: runtime — read at SessionClose invocation, not deploy time
relationship: Content payload — wrapped in vault-entry-schema.json envelope before vault write

platform.description: The AI platform this session ran on. Hardcoded per plugin at generation time — never inferred at runtime.

loop.description: Which refinement loop this summary feeds. Maps to memory_entries.loop. Default 'plugin' for auto-generated session summaries — use 'user' for summaries about user preferences/patterns, 'client' for org-level facts.

primary_topic.description: One sentence describing the main thing worked on this session

key_decisions.description: 2-6 decisions made this session that will affect future sessions. Choices and conclusions, not tactical steps.

corrections.description: Factual errors caught and corrected, or misunderstandings resolved this session

new_knowledge.description: Things discovered this session that weren't known before — patterns, constraints, user preferences, architectural insights

open_threads.description: Unresolved questions or tasks explicitly left for a future session

narrative.description: 100-300 word plain-language summary of the session — what happened, why it mattered, what changed. Written as if briefing a colleague who missed the session.

notes: Never include PII, credentials, or confidential data in session summaries
Corrections and new_knowledge are optional but highly valuable for the compounding loop
This schema is the content — the envelope (vault-entry-schema.json) adds metadata like entry_id, plugin, and timestamp
